summary: Round 11 reopens HNSW from the round-10 authority hard stop rather than from another generic same-schema parity claim. Round 10 ended with Rust HNSW at 1831.504 qps versus native 8900.882 qps, a 4.86x gap, and no attributable Rust-side win from layer0 slab locality. The new tracked hypothesis is filtered_bruteforce_fallback: align high-filter-ratio bitset KNN search with native conditional brute-force policy, then verify on the authority lane whether filtered-query performance improves without disturbing HNSW correctness contracts.
action: Keep the historical HNSW family verdict unchanged, freeze the round-10 authority hard stop plus the promoted filtered-fallback screen evidence as the round-11 baseline, and require a fresh authority rerun before making any broader HNSW claim.
